task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the project directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the current working directory as /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/inventory or a file explorer open to this path.

task_completion.description: Agent modifies the Docker Compose configuration to correctly mount the initialization script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing docker-compose.yml with a volume mapping added for the database service, mapping init.sql to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ (e.g., ./init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql).

task_completion.description: Agent removes the old database volume so the initialization script can run on a fresh database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'docker compose down -v', 'docker volume rm inventory_pgdata', or 'docker system prune -v' being executed.

task_completion.description: Agent starts the Docker Compose services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'docker compose up' or 'docker compose up -d' and the containers (including inventory_db) starting.

task_completion.description: Agent verifies that the 'products' table was successfully created in the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a psql command executed against the inventory_db container (e.g., docker exec -it inventory_db psql -U postgres) and the output displaying the 'products' table in the relation list.

integrity.description: Verify that Docker commands were actually executed in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows standard Docker output logs (e.g., pulling images, creating networks, container logs) rather than just echoed text.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration file was edited using a real text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a recognized text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) being used to modify docker-compose.yml, not just a cat command or echo.

integrity.description: Verify that the database table check is from a real PostgreSQL prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the standard psql prompt (e.g., 'postgres=#') and authentic tabular output for the '\dt' or 'SELECT' command.
